methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $27M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 2 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Astro Mechanica is a Series A-stage company in the Supersonic & Hypersonic sector, having raised $27M+ to date. The broader comp universe for this sector includes two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and two material private peers (Boom Supersonic, Hermeus), though narrow peer comparables at identical stage and valuation tier are limited.

Given the early-stage nature and sector specificity, only the stage-progression method is reliably applicable here. Using total raised ($27M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression to next-stage rounds yields an implied valuation of $54M. The peer-median approach cannot be applied due to insufficient same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier datapoints within the Supersonic & Hypersonic vertical—triangulation via public comp multiples would require additional granular revenue or growth data not present in this dataset.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $46M to $62M, centered on $54M. This range reflects a ±15% band around the stage-progression result, acknowledging the single-method dependency. Key uncertainties include the applicability of standard Series A multipliers to frontier aerospace technology, the limited liquidity and opacity of comparable private transactions, and the absence of revenue or contract-backed valuation anchors. Additional data on customer traction, funding velocity, or technical milestones would improve precision.
